pi_items.verified_value: LineString is correct. Roads dataset (ne_10m_roads.shp) contains only LineString geometries. A spatial intersection of LineString roads with Polygon parks will produce LineString (or MultiLineString) output.
pi_items.source: Examined roads shapefile header. Natural Earth documentation confirms roads are PolyLine/LineString type. Verified via shapefile header reading.
Cannot determine without full spatial intersection query between 56,600 roads globally and ~400 parks (US-only). However, range appears reasonable given dataset sizes.

Contains 398 US National Park Service units split into area (61), line (29), point (315), and scale_rank (varies) shapefiles.
pi_items.source: Verified by downloading from naciscdn.org. Natural Earth documentation states '398 parks, including the latest additions'. All datasets version 5.0.0 per VERSION.txt files. Parks are US-only as documented.

pi_items.verified_value: Roads shapefile contains attributes: scalerank, featurecla, type, sov_a3, name, namealt, routeraw, and many others (32 total fields). Verifier checks for presence of at least 2 of: [type, name, sov_a3, scalerank] - all 4 are present.
pi_items.source: Read DBF headers from ne_10m_roads.dbf using binary file parsing. All expected attributes confirmed.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real Natural Earth v5.0.0 data. Roads data is valid: ne_10m_roads.shp contains 56,600 LineString features with preserved attributes (type, name, sov_a3, scalerank). However, the metadata path for parks data is incorrect—it specifies 'ne_10m_parks_and_protected_lands.shp' which does not exist. The actual Natural Earth distribution contains separate shapefiles: _area (61 polygons), _line (29 lines), _point (315 points) for 398 US National Park Service units. The expected output geometry type (LineString) is correct for road features. Feature count bounds (50-20000) cannot be independently verified without performing the spatial intersection, but appear reasonable given that roads globally number 56,600 and parks are US-only (~400 features).
